message:
fix(sync): disable JSONL sync exports in dolt-native mode (bd-zlih1)

The previous fix (69bf2c96) only disabled autoflush JSONL writes but
sync operations (bd sync, bd sync --flush-only, etc.) still wrote JSONL
via exportToJSONL/exportToJSONLDeferred functions.

This fix adds ShouldExportJSONL() checks to all sync export functions:
- exportToJSONL()
- exportToJSONLDeferred()
- exportToJSONLIncrementalDeferred()

In dolt-native mode, JSONL is now completely disabled:
- No autoflush writes (fixed in 69bf2c96)
- No sync exports (this commit)
- No incremental exports (this commit)

Also updated the SyncModeDoltNative description from "JSONL export-only
(backup)" to "no JSONL writes" to reflect the new behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: beads/crew/emma
Rig: beads
Role: crew

files.patch:
@@ -20,7 +20,7 @@ const (
 	// Provides immediate persistence but more git noise.
 	SyncModeRealtime = string(config.SyncModeRealtime)
 
-	// SyncModeDoltNative uses Dolt remotes for sync, JSONL export-only (backup).
+	// SyncModeDoltNative uses Dolt remotes for sync, no JSONL writes.
 	// Requires Dolt backend and configured Dolt remote.
 	SyncModeDoltNative = string(config.SyncModeDoltNative)
 
@@ -141,7 +141,7 @@ func SyncModeDescription(mode string) string {
 	case SyncModeRealtime:
 		return "JSONL exported on every change"
 	case SyncModeDoltNative:
-		return "Dolt remotes for sync, JSONL export-only (backup)"
+		return "Dolt remotes for sync, no JSONL writes"
 	case SyncModeBeltAndSuspenders:
 		return "Both Dolt remotes and JSONL"
 	default:
